calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lina Balciunas Cockrell, Esq. of Messing Adam & Jasmine LLP, T:916.446.5297, for petitioner/moving party;
Shanna McDaniel, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, T:916.210.7874, for real parties in interest.

RULING: The Court's tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the tentative ruling was adopted on PETITIONER TIMOTHY EDGMON's Motion for Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.

Petitioner Edgmon's Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandamus is denied. Respondent's factual findings are reviewed under the substantial evidence standard and its legal determinations are subject to de novo review. (See Yancey v. State Personnel Bd. (1985) 167 Cal.App.3d 478, 482; Shafer v. Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1388, 1396.)

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Counsel for real parties in interest to submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Petitioner'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER TIMOTHY EDGMON's Motion To Lift Stay is OFF CALENDAR as moot. On October 10, 2018, the court signed the parties' stipulation and order that lifts the stay. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection's Motion to Change Venue is denied and its alternative motion to stay is granted. San Francisco County is a proper venue under CCP Section 401 and CAL FIRE has not demonstrated that a transfer is necessary to further the ends of justice in this writ case. The doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction requires that this case be stayed pending the conclusion of the case filed in Sacramento County which itself is stayed pending decision on the appeal to the Third District. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
